audit_status.source_base: Hebrew 2 Samuel chapter 17, first-pass draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: Reviewed for assassination planning, Hushai's counterstrategy, total-war imagery, providence framing, priests and young messengers, unnamed girl's intelligence work, Bahurim woman's concealment, Ahithophel's suicide, family order, burial, military genealogy, and material support for hungry refugees.

verses.restored: Ahithophel said to Absalom, Please let me choose twelve thousand men. I will arise and pursue David tonight.
I will come upon him while he is weary and weak-handed, terrify him, and all people with him will flee. I will strike king alone.
I will return all people to you. Return of all depends on man you seek; all people will be at peace.
Word was right in eyes of Absalom and in eyes of all elders of Israel.
Absalom said, Please call also Hushai the Archite, and let us hear what is in his mouth as well.
Hushai came to Absalom. Absalom spoke to him, saying, According to this word Ahithophel spoke. Shall we do his word? If not, you speak.
Hushai said to Absalom, Counsel Ahithophel counseled is not good this time.
Hushai said, You know your father and his men, that they are mighty and bitter in soul, like bear robbed of cubs in field. Your father is a man of war and will not lodge with people.
Look, now he hides in one of pits or in one place. When some fall at first, hearer will hear and say, There is a slaughter among people following Absalom.
Even son of strength whose heart is like heart of lion will utterly melt, because all Israel knows your father is mighty and those with him are sons of strength.
Therefore I counsel: let all Israel surely gather to you, from Dan to Beersheba, like sand by sea for multitude, and you go to battle in your person.
We will come upon him in one of places where he is found and settle upon him as dew falls upon ground. We will not leave of him and all men with him even one.
If he gathers into a city, all Israel will carry ropes to that city and drag it to wadi until not even a pebble is found there.

Absalom and all men of Israel said, Counsel of Hushai the Archite is better than counsel of Ahithophel. YHWH had commanded to frustrate good counsel of Ahithophel so YHWH might bring evil upon Absalom.
Hushai said to Zadok and Abiathar the priests, Thus and thus Ahithophel counseled Absalom and elders of Israel, and thus and thus I counseled.
Now send quickly and tell David, saying, Do not lodge tonight at crossings of wilderness. Crossing, cross over, lest king and all people with him be swallowed.
Jonathan and Ahimaaz were staying at En-rogel because they could not be seen entering city. A young woman/servant girl went and told them, and they went and told king David.
A young man saw them and told Absalom. They both went quickly and came to house of a man in Bahurim who had a well in courtyard. They went down into it.
Woman took covering and spread it over mouth of well, and spread crushed grain upon it, so thing was not known.
Servants of Absalom came to woman at house and said, Where are Ahimaaz and Jonathan? Woman said to them, They crossed over brook of water. They sought and did not find, and returned to Jerusalem.
After they went, messengers came up from well, went, and told king David. They said to David, Arise and quickly cross water, because thus Ahithophel counseled against you.
David arose and all people with him, and they crossed Jordan. By light of morning not one was missing who had not crossed Jordan.

Ahithophel saw his counsel was not done. He saddled donkey, arose, went to his house in his city, commanded/ordered his household, strangled/hanged himself, died, and was buried in grave of his father.
David came to Mahanaim, and Absalom crossed Jordan, he and every man of Israel with him.
Absalom placed Amasa over army in place of Joab. Amasa was son of a man named Ithra the Israelite/Ishmaelite, who came to Abigail daughter of Nahash, sister of Zeruiah mother of Joab.
Israel and Absalom camped in land of Gilead.
When David came to Mahanaim, Shobi son of Nahash from Rabbah of children of Ammon, Machir son of Ammiel from Lo-debar, and Barzillai the Gileadite from Rogelim came.
They brought beds, basins, pottery, wheat, barley, flour, roasted grain, beans, lentils, and roasted pulse,
honey, curds, sheep, and cheese of cattle for David and people with him to eat, because they said, People are hungry, weary, and thirsty in wilderness.

translation_notes: Ahithophel's plan is targeted assassination designed to exploit exhaustion and separate a leader from followers. Hushai counters it with an even larger total-war fantasy to delay action.
Hushai's counsel is strategically deceptive. Its imagery includes annihilating every follower and destroying a sheltering city; delay does not make the proposal ethically humane.
Verse 14 calls Ahithophel's counsel 'good' tactically while framing its defeat as Divine purpose. Tactical effectiveness and moral goodness must not be confused.
Priests, their sons, an unnamed girl, a young observer, and a Bahurim woman become an intelligence network. The unnamed women perform decisive and dangerous work.
The woman lies to armed searchers to protect people hidden in her well. Protective deception under lethal threat is ethically distinct from exploitative dishonesty.

Ahithophel dies by suicide after arranging his household. The text reports planning and death without giving his inner state; it must never be romanticized as honor, control, wisdom, or an appropriate response to humiliation.
Amasa's genealogy varies across witnesses, including Israelite/Ishmaelite. Ethnic variants must not become contempt.
The chapter ends not with strategy but with food, beds, vessels, and care for hungry, exhausted, thirsty people. Material support sustains survival.
safety_note: Maximum suicide-misuse safeguards: Do not praise or imitate Ahithophel's death, present suicide as dignified response to rejection, or assume motives not stated. In real life, suicidal crisis calls for immediate human contact, safety, and professional support. Also protect the unnamed women and young messengers from being romanticized as expendable intelligence assets.
